metadata.what_it_measures: Four-week moving average of first-time unemployment insurance filings. DOL. Weekly, Thursday 8:30 ET. Smooths weekly noise in raw claims.
metadata.why_it_matters: High-frequency labor market read. Rising claims = layoffs accelerating. The 4-week MA is the most useful version; raw claims are too volatile. Claims >350k historically associated with recession conditions.
metadata.scoring_rule: z = (current 4wMA - trailing 3y mean) / trailing 3y stddev momentum = (current 4wMA - 4wMA 8 weeks ago) / 4wMA 8 weeks ago raw_score = -50*z - 100*momentum (high & rising = bearish) clamp to [-100, +100]
